  [prec-002] IF modifying/exchanging an item → THEN ensure the replacement variant's availability is true in get_product_details before proposing it
  [prec-003] IF performing any charge or refund → THEN ensure the specified payment_method_id exists in get_user_details(payment_methods). For refunds, default to the original payment method from the order's payment_history. However, if the customer explicitly requests a different payment method that exists in their account and the system supports applying the charge/refund to that method (e.g., for exchanges or price differences), honor the customer's request and use the specified payment_method_id, documenting the selection in the tool call.
  [prec-004] IF no explicit user confirmation is received after a proposed change → THEN do not execute exchange/return/modify/cancel actions. EXCEPTION: treat an unambiguous user instruction to perform the action (for example: 'Cancel this order', 'No longer needed', 'Please modify to X') or a clearly stated cancellation reason as sufficient confirmation to proceed without an additional 'Yes' prompt.
  [prec-005] IF a user requests returns and exchanges in the same request → THEN do not execute both actions simultaneously; ask the user to choose which action to perform first (process them as separate requests).
  [prec-006] IF changing a user's account address → THEN note that this does NOT retroactively change the shipping address on already processed or delivered orders; order shipping addresses must be updated separately and only pending orders can have their shipping address modified. The assistant must inform the user which orders (by order id) were updated and which orders could not be updated due to status.
  [prec-007] IF an exchange or modification requires charging a payment method that may have limited balance (e.g., a gift card) → THEN check the payment_method's available balance (from get_user_details/payment method metadata) before executing. If the balance is insufficient to cover any required charge, DO NOT execute the exchange/modification. Inform the user of the insufficient balance, provide the exact shortfall amount, and offer options: (a) provide an alternate payment method, (b) pause/hold the requested change until the user supplies payment, or (c) cancel the change request. Always use the original payment method for refunds.
  [prec-008] IF an order's status is cancelled → THEN the order cannot be reinstated via automated return/modify/cancel/exchange tools; refuse automated reinstatement and escalate to human agents (transfer_to_human_agents) for manual review/possible reinstatement.
  [prec-009] IF an order status is pending AND the user requests cancellation of only some items in that pending order → THEN do not perform partial cancellations; pending orders may be modified (change variants/quantities) using modify_pending_order_items or cancelled in full using cancel_pending_order. Partial-item returns/cancellations are only allowed for delivered orders.
  [prec-010] IF an order already has an active return_requested or exchange_requested state → THEN do not initiate additional return or exchange requests for the same order until the existing request is resolved; inform the user of the existing request and next steps.
  [prec-011] IF a user requests cancellation for only part of a pending order → THEN inform the user that partial cancellations are not supported (or per-system policy), and offer the appropriate alternative (use modify_pending_order_items to remove/replace items if supported, or cancel the entire order if the system supports it).
  [prec-012] IF an address modification request is received → THEN check the order status: delivered orders' shipping addresses cannot be changed; only pending/processed (modifiable) orders can have their shipping address updated. Inform the user which orders cannot be changed and proceed only for eligible orders.

all_deltas.content: IF no explicit user confirmation is received after a proposed change → THEN do not execute exchange/return/modify/cancel actions (ask for an explicit affirmative confirmation; acceptable confirmations include clear affirmative phrases such as 'Yes', 'Please go ahead', 'Proceed', 'Charge my card', 'I’m ready', or similar explicit consent).
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show users giving varied explicit affirmative phrasings (not always the literal word 'Yes') before the agent proceeded; the rule should accept clear affirmative consent rather than a single literal token.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests an account or order address change → THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the account address and call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, ...) for each pending order to update shipping addresses; present the updated address(es) to the user and confirm the change.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents updated both the user account address and all pending orders in a single flow (conversation 41); playbook lacks an explicit rule for performing both user and pending-order address updates.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests returns and exchanges in the same request → THEN do not execute both actions simultaneously; ask the user to choose which action to perform first (process them as separate requests).
all_deltas.rationale: A trajectory showed the agent refusing to process a return and exchanges at the same time and asking the user to pick one (conversation 19); playbook should explicitly prohibit simultaneous return+exchange processing.

all_deltas.content: IF preparing to execute a change (exchange, return, modification, cancellation) → THEN present the exact actions to the user including order id, old item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or return/cancel), price(s), and payment_method_id to be used and ask for explicit confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue'). Exception: if the user in the same message explicitly requests the change (for example, 'Please update my address to X' or 'Please cancel these orders now'), the assistant may proceed without an additional 'Yes' prompt, but must still summarize the exact actions it will take (order ids, item ids, prices, and payment methods) before executing and confirm the summary with the user.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents proceeding when users explicitly requested changes in the same message (e.g., account/address update) without a separate 'Yes' prompt; to reflect that while preserving the requirement to summarize actions, conf-001 should allow proceeding on explicit user instruction.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests an update to their account shipping address → THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the account address. IF a user requests shipping-address updates for orders → THEN only modify addresses for orders with status 'pending' using modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip). When performing these updates, communicate which orders were successfully updated and which could not be (e.g., processed/delivered orders).
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories include direct use of modify_user_address and modify_pending_order_address and the assistant behavior that account updates are separate from order shipping addresses; the playbook lacked explicit rules about these tools and the distinction.

all_deltas.content: IF changing a user's account address → THEN note that this does NOT retroactively change the shipping address on already processed or delivered orders; order shipping addresses must be updated separately and only pending orders can have their shipping address modified. The assistant must inform the user which orders (by order id) were updated and which orders could not be updated due to status.
all_deltas.rationale: In practice assistants informed users that only pending orders' addresses could be changed and updated pending orders separately; the playbook should explicitly require this communication and precondition.

all_deltas.content: IF order status is pending AND user requests an address update → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the shipping address. Before executing, present the exact new address that will be applied (or state 'use address on file' if requested) and ask for explicit confirmation from the user.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents using modify_pending_order_address to update shipping on pending orders (conversation 1). The playbook had no explicit action rule for address updates or the requirement to present the exact new address for confirmation.

all_deltas.content: IF an exchange or modification requires charging a payment method that may have limited balance (e.g., a gift card) → THEN check the payment_method's available balance (from get_user_details/payment method metadata) before executing. If the balance is insufficient to cover any required charge, DO NOT execute the exchange/modification. Inform the user of the insufficient balance, provide the exact shortfall amount, and offer options: (a) provide an alternate payment method, (b) pause/hold the requested change until the user supplies payment, or (c) cancel the change request. Always use the original payment method for refunds.
all_deltas.rationale: A trajectory attempted an exchange paid with a gift card that lacked sufficient balance; the system returned an error and the user asked to hold the exchange (conversation 3). The playbook lacked a precondition about checking payment method balance and handling insufficient funds.

all_deltas.content: IF an order contains multiple similar or identical items (duplicate item_ids or same product repeated) AND the user references 'one of them' or is ambiguous about which unit to act on → THEN ask the user to specify which item they mean (by item options, item_id index, shipping address, or other distinguishing detail) before proceeding with returns/exchanges/modifications.
all_deltas.rationale: When orders contain duplicate/identical items, agents must clarify which specific unit to process; in conversation 5 the agent explicitly asked which vacuum the user wanted to return, demonstrating the need for a clarifying step.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests to change the shipping address for a pending order → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update that order's shipping address. IF the user also asks to update their account default address (or requests the account address to match an order's shipping address) → THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) after confirming with the user.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents updated a pending order's shipping address and the user's account address (modify_pending_order_address and modify_user_address were used) when the user asked the account be set to the order address.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests multiple changes (exchanges/modifications/returns) and only confirms some of the proposed replacements/options → THEN proceed only with the actions the user explicitly confirmed (execute tools for confirmed items) and leave unconfirmed items unchanged; confirm the exact actions and payment method for the confirmed subset before executing.
all_deltas.rationale: Users requested multiple item changes but declined some (e.g., declined keyboard exchange but proceeded with thermostat). The agent proceeded only with the confirmed item.

all_deltas.content: IF preparing to execute a change (exchange, return, modification, cancellation) → THEN present the exact actions to the user including order id, old item_ids, proposed new item_ids (or return/cancel), price(s), and payment_method_id to be used and ask for explicit confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue'). Confirmation may be collected in steps (for example: confirm item replacements/options first, then confirm which payment method to use), but a clear final explicit confirmation ('Yes' or equivalent) covering the full action must be obtained before executing the exchange/return/modify/cancel.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show confirmations collected in multiple steps (items first, then payment method) before execution. The rule is refined to allow multi-step confirmation while still requiring a final explicit OK before performing actions.

all_deltas.content: IF order status is pending AND user requests a shipping address change → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the order's shipping address.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents updated the shipping address for a pending order using modify_pending_order_address; playbook lacked an explicit rule for address changes on pending orders.

all_deltas.content: IF find_user_id_by_name_zip(...) returns no user → THEN ask the user for their email and call find_user_id_by_email(email) to attempt to locate the account as a fallback.
all_deltas.rationale: When name+zip lookup failed, agents correctly asked for the email and used it to find the user; the playbook should specify this fallback lookup flow.

all_deltas.content: IF an order's status is cancelled → THEN the order cannot be reinstated via automated return/modify/cancel/exchange tools; refuse automated reinstatement and escalate to human agents (transfer_to_human_agents) for manual review/possible reinstatement.
all_deltas.rationale: A user requested undoing a cancellation and the agent escalated to human agents; playbook should explicitly prohibit automated reinstatement of cancelled orders and require escalation.

all_deltas.content: IF a user provides a customer ID / user_id directly → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve the user's email, payment methods, address, and list of order IDs (accept the provided user_id as valid identifying info in lieu of name+zip or email).
all_deltas.rationale: Users sometimes supply their customer ID directly; the agent should accept and use it to find the account rather than requiring name/zip or email again.

all_deltas.content: IF no explicit user confirmation is received after a proposed change → THEN do not execute exchange/return/modify/cancel actions; require an explicit affirmative confirmation from the user before proceeding. Acceptable confirmations include 'Yes' and equivalent clear affirmative phrases (e.g., 'Yes', 'Yep', 'That works', 'Please go ahead', 'Proceed', 'Okay'). If the user's response is ambiguous, ask them to explicitly confirm before executing.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents proceeded when users gave clear affirmative confirmations that were not literally the word 'Yes'. Update rule to accept common affirmative responses while preserving the requirement for an explicit confirmation.

all_deltas.content: IF order status is pending AND user requests cancellation → THEN use cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) to cancel and issue refund to the original payment method. Note: cancel_pending_order applies to the entire pending order; partial-item cancellations on pending orders are not supported. If the user requests cancelling only some items, inform them that partial cancellation of a pending order isn't available and offer alternatives (cancel the entire order, or modify/replace items if supported while pending).
all_deltas.rationale: In practice agents declined partial cancellations for pending orders and required whole-order cancellation; clarify this in the playbook and provide alternatives to offer the user.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests to split payment across multiple payment methods for a single order → THEN refuse the request and inform the user that a single payment method must be used for that order; offer alternatives such as removing items to reduce the total, using a different single payment method, or placing a new separate order to use a different card.
all_deltas.rationale: Users may ask to split payment across cards but the system processes a single payment method per order; agents should refuse and offer workable alternatives.

all_deltas.content: IF an order status is pending AND the user requests cancellation of only some items in that pending order → THEN do not perform partial cancellations; pending orders may be modified (change variants/quantities) using modify_pending_order_items or cancelled in full using cancel_pending_order. Partial-item returns/cancellations are only allowed for delivered orders.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show that individual-item cancellations are not permitted on pending orders; clarify allowed actions for pending orders.

all_deltas.content: IF the user requests multiple returns/exchanges across multiple orders → THEN compute and clearly present the aggregated total refund or total charge (net) and list which payment method(s) each amount will be applied to (e.g., 'Total refund would be $X to your [payment method(s)]'), and confirm with the user.
all_deltas.rationale: Users expect a single summed total when multiple returns/refunds are processed across orders; agents should provide an aggregated summary and payment-method mapping.

all_deltas.content: IF order status is pending AND user requests an address change → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the shipping address before performing other pending-order actions; present the updated address to the user and ask for explicit confirmation.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show the assistant used modify_pending_order_address for pending-order address updates (task_id=96) but the playbook lacked a rule for this action.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests an aggregate total or spending summary for a product type/category across their past orders → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to obtain order IDs, call get_order_details(order_id) for each order, filter items by the requested product_id(s) or product name, sum the item prices, and present the total with an optional breakdown by order and item.
all_deltas.rationale: A user asked for the total spent on grills and the assistant computed a sum across past orders (task_id=76); the playbook lacks guidance for aggregated historical spending requests.

all_deltas.content: IF preparing to execute a change (exchange, return, modification, cancellation) → THEN present the exact actions to the user including order id, old item_ids, proposed new item_ids (or return/cancel), price(s), any price difference (exact charge or refund amount), and the payment_method_id that will be used; show how refunds/charges will be applied (e.g., 'refund would be $X to your [payment method]' or 'charge would be $Y to your [payment method]') and ask for explicit confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue').
all_deltas.rationale: Some trajectories requested confirmation without showing the price difference or specifying how refunds/charges would be applied prior to execution (see task_ids 7 and 96); clarifying that price differences and refund/charge routing must be shown before asking for 'Yes' reduces ambiguity and ensures consistency with later payment actions.

all_deltas.content: IF multiple exchanges/returns/modifications are requested across one or more orders → THEN present a per-order breakdown listing order_id, old item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or return/cancel), per-item and per-order price(s), the price difference for each order, the payment_method_id to be used for each charge/refund, and the total net charge or refund across all orders; require explicit user confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue') before executing any tool calls.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show multi-order exchanges were executed without a consolidated per-order and total summary and explicit confirmation (Conversation 1). Agents need to present aggregated details and get explicit consent when multiple actions span orders.

all_deltas.content: DO NOT request identifying information (name/zip/email) for general product inquiries, browsing, or availability requests. Only prompt for identifying info when the user requests account-specific actions (account/order changes, returns, exchanges, cancellations, or other actions that require accessing the user's account or orders).
all_deltas.rationale: Agents asked for PII when the user only requested product availability/cheapest item (Conversation 5). This avoids unnecessary collection of identifying information.

all_deltas.content: IF order status is pending AND user requests a shipping address change → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the shipping address for the pending order and present the updated shipping details to the user for confirmation.
all_deltas.rationale: A pending-order address change was performed in the trajectories (Conversation 4), so a specific action rule for modifying addresses on pending orders is needed.

all_deltas.content: IF an order already has an active return_requested or exchange_requested state → THEN do not initiate additional return or exchange requests for the same order until the existing request is resolved; inform the user of the existing request and next steps.
all_deltas.rationale: A return was already requested on an order and the agent refused a second return (Conversation 2). The playbook should explicitly forbid multiple concurrent return/exchange requests on the same order.

all_deltas.content: IF preparing to execute a change (exchange, return, modification, cancellation) → THEN present the exact actions to the user including order id, old item_ids, proposed new item_ids (or return/cancel), per-item and per-order price(s), any price differences, the payment_method_id(s) to be used, and the total net charge or refund across affected orders; ask for explicit confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue'). Do not execute any exchange/return/modify/cancel actions until the user provides explicit confirmation referencing the presented summary.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show actions (especially multi-order exchanges) were sometimes executed without a clear itemized summary and explicit confirmation of the exact amounts and payment method (Conversation 1). Clarify that the summary must include price differences and totals and that explicit confirmation is required before execution.

all_deltas.content: IF a payment_method of type gift_card is used for a modification or pending-order charge → THEN deduct the charged amount from the gift_card balance, update the user's payment_methods balance, and report the remaining gift card balance to the user as part of the confirmation communication after the action is prepared and/or executed.
all_deltas.rationale: Users expect to see updated gift card balances after charges for modifications (Conversation 3). The system must update and communicate gift card balances when they are used for charges.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests cancellation for only part of a pending order → THEN inform the user that partial cancellations are not supported (or per-system policy), and offer the appropriate alternative (use modify_pending_order_items to remove/replace items if supported, or cancel the entire order if the system supports it).
all_deltas.rationale: A user attempted to cancel part of a pending order and the agent indicated partial cancellation was not available, then used an address update instead (Conversation 4). The playbook should explicitly state the policy and alternatives for partial cancellations of pending orders.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests account/order changes or returns/exchanges → THEN ask for identifying info: name and zip OR email (at least one is required). If the user provides both name/zip and email, accept either and prefer email for user lookup. Proceed with lookup using the identifier(s) provided.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents accepted and used email when users provided both name and email; the original 'exactly one' wording was too strict and blocked normal variations in user-provided identifiers.

all_deltas.content: IF performing any charge or refund → THEN ensure the specified payment_method_id exists in get_user_details(payment_methods). By default, refunds should be returned to the original payment method from the order's payment_history. If the user explicitly requests a different payment method that exists on their account, the agent may use that specified payment method for charges or refunds. Do not accept requests to refund to a different order's payment method (see acti-007).
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show users sometimes request refunds/exchange payments to a different payment method on their account (not a different order's payment method) and agents honored those requests; the rule should allow that when the payment method exists on the user's account while keeping the default as original-method refunds.

all_deltas.content: IF preparing to execute a change (exchange, return, modification, cancellation) → THEN present the exact actions to the user including order id, old item_ids, proposed new item_ids (or return/cancel), price(s), and payment_method_id to be used and ask for explicit confirmation. If the user has already explicitly requested the action in clear terms (e.g., 'Please return X', 'Cancel order #W...', 'Please process the exchange for...'), treat that explicit request as confirmation and proceed; otherwise wait for an explicit 'Yes' or equivalent confirmation before executing the change.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents executed actions when users explicitly asked to proceed (e.g., 'Please help me return...') without a separate 'Yes' prompt. The rule should distinguish between a clear user instruction (which can be treated as confirmation) and situations that require an explicit confirmation prompt.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests updating shipping address for pending orders or their account → THEN for each pending order call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update that order's shipping address; and call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the account's default address. Confirm which specific orders the user wants changed and only modify orders with modifiable statuses (see precondition rules).
all_deltas.rationale: Agents performed modify_pending_order_address and modify_user_address to update pending orders and the account address; the playbook lacked explicit address-modification actions.

all_deltas.content: IF an address modification request is received → THEN check the order status: delivered orders' shipping addresses cannot be changed; only pending/processed (modifiable) orders can have their shipping address updated. Inform the user which orders cannot be changed and proceed only for eligible orders.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents informed users that delivered orders' addresses cannot be changed and only pending orders were modifiable; this should be codified as a precondition.

all_deltas.content: IF a user asks a question or makes a request that is unrelated to their account, orders, returns, or exchanges → THEN do not request identifying information (name/zip/email). Only request identifying info when it is needed to look up or act on the user's account/orders.
all_deltas.rationale: In conversation 3 the assistant requested name/zip for a general (poem) question — agents must not ask for personal identifying info unless needed to perform account/order actions.

all_deltas.content: IF a user is unsure which payment method they used for an order → THEN retrieve the order details and read payment_history to identify the original payment_method_id to use for refunds/charges instead of asking the user to supply the payment method.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents asked users to name their payment card even when the order's payment history contains the original payment method (seen in conversation 1); look up the original payment method from order data when available.

all_deltas.content: IF preparing to execute a change (exchange, return, modification, cancellation) → THEN present the exact actions to the user including order id, old item_ids, proposed new item_ids (or return/cancel), price(s), and payment_method_id to be used and ask for explicit confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue'). If the user has already given an explicit instruction to proceed for the exact requested action (for example, 'Please go ahead and cancel both orders for me'), treat that instruction as the required explicit confirmation and proceed only after ensuring the user understood the exact actions that will be taken.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents must present exact actions and require explicit confirmation before executing; however, an earlier unambiguous user request to proceed should be accepted as confirmation (seen in conversation 2), while agents must not proceed without confirmation when no explicit proceed instruction was given (seen in conversation 1).

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests refunds/returns to be applied to a different payment method than the original order's payment_history → THEN refuse and state that refunds/returns must go to the original payment method for that order, and IF the user insists or requests further assistance → THEN offer to escalate/transfer the request to a human agent for review.
all_deltas.rationale: The playbook already requires refusal for alternate payment methods, but agents should also offer escalation when a customer insists (observed in conversation 4 where the user requested Amex but original was PayPal).

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests account/order changes or returns/exchanges → THEN ask for identifying info: name and zip OR email (at least one required). If both are provided, prefer using email to locate the account (call find_user_id_by_email); if only name and zip are provided, call find_user_id_by_name_zip.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents accepted both name+email from users and used the email to find the account; the original 'exactly one' requirement is too strict and prevented handling cases where users supply both.

all_deltas.content: IF order status is pending AND user requests a shipping address change → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the shipping address on the pending order.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents updated the shipping address on pending orders via modify_pending_order_address; the playbook lacks an explicit rule for address changes on pending orders.

all_deltas.content: IF an action involves returned or exchanged items → THEN verify the order's status: delivered orders may be returned or exchanged; pending orders must be modified or cancelled; cancelled orders cannot be returned or exchanged. (Do not use delivered-return/exchange tools on pending or cancelled orders.)
all_deltas.rationale: Agents refused returns for items in cancelled orders and explicitly stated cancelled items cannot be returned; the existing rule did not mention cancelled orders.

all_deltas.content: IF preparing to execute a change (exchange, return, modification, cancellation) → THEN present the exact actions to the user including order id, old item_ids, proposed new item_ids (or return/cancel), price(s), and payment_method_id to be used and ask for explicit confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue'). Exception: if the user explicitly and unambiguously requests that you proceed (for example, a clear instruction like 'Please do it' or 'Yes, proceed'), you may perform the requested action without an additional confirmation prompt, but you must still summarize the exact actions, prices (including any price differences), and the payment method either immediately before executing the tool call or immediately after executing and before ending the interaction.
all_deltas.rationale: In multiple conversations agents executed returns/modifications immediately after a user's clear request without prompting for an extra 'Yes' confirmation — the rule should allow proceeding on explicit user instruction while ensuring users still receive a clear summary of actions and payment details.

all_deltas.content: IF requested returns/exchanges include items from more than one order → THEN perform separate return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items calls per order, and use the original payment method from each respective order's payment_history for refunds/charges.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents processed returns across multiple orders by making separate tool calls and refunded each order to its original payment method; the playbook should explicitly require per-order tool calls and per-order original payment usage for multi-order returns.

all_deltas.content: IF performing any charge or refund → THEN ensure the specified payment_method_id exists in get_user_details(payment_methods). For refunds, default to the original payment method from the order's payment_history. However, if the customer explicitly requests a different payment method that exists in their account and the system supports applying the charge/refund to that method (e.g., for exchanges or price differences), honor the customer's request and use the specified payment_method_id, documenting the selection in the tool call.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show exchanges/price-difference adjustments being applied to alternate user-owned payment methods (and gift cards) when the customer explicitly requested it. The rule must allow alternate user-owned methods when supported while preserving the default of the original payment method for refunds.

all_deltas.content: IF a user explicitly requests refunds to a different payment method → THEN verify the requested payment_method_id belongs to the same user (exists in get_user_details). If it does and system policy permits applying refunds/charges to that method (e.g., for exchanges/price differences), honor the request. Refuse requests that attempt refunds to payment methods not in the user's account, to another customer's payment method, or to payment methods that the system does not support for refunds.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show customers asking to use a different user-owned payment method (e.g., a specific credit card or gift card) and having the system apply exchanges/charges to that method. The rule should permit user-owned alternate methods when valid and supported, while still preventing refunds to invalid or other-users' methods.

all_deltas.content: IF order status is pending AND user requests a shipping address update → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the order's shipping address, call get_order_details(order_id) after the update to confirm the new address and any price/shipping changes, and present the updated order summary to the user for confirmation.
all_deltas.rationale: A pending-order address change (modify_pending_order_address) was used in the trajectories; playbook lacked an explicit rule for updating shipping addresses on pending orders.

all_deltas.content: IF preparing to execute a change (exchange, return, modification, cancellation) → THEN present the exact actions to the user including order id, old item_ids, proposed new item_ids (or return/cancel), price(s), and payment_method_id to be used and ask for explicit confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue'). If the user has already provided an unambiguous, explicit instruction authorizing the requested change earlier in the conversation (for example, they asked to proceed with the specific exchange/modification and specified preferences), restate the exact actions being taken and proceed without an additional 'Yes' only if the prior message clearly authorized proceeding.
all_deltas.rationale: In some trajectories the user gave a clear, explicit instruction up front and the agent proceeded after verification steps without a separate 'Yes' confirmation. The rule should require confirmation normally but allow proceeding when the user has already explicitly authorized the exact change.

all_deltas.content: IF no explicit user confirmation is received after a proposed change → THEN do not execute exchange/return/modify/cancel actions (ask for explicit 'Yes' to proceed). Exception: if the user previously gave an unambiguous, explicit instruction in the same conversation authorizing the requested change (including specifying desired replacement items and payment method), the agent may proceed after restating the actions to be executed.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories demonstrate cases where users issued an explicit instruction earlier (so no additional 'Yes' was requested before proceeding). The precondition should allow proceeding when prior unambiguous consent exists, but otherwise require explicit confirmation.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests account/order changes or returns/exchanges → THEN ask for identifying info: name and zip OR email (at least one of these is required). If the user provides both name+zip and email, prefer find_user_id_by_email with the email; otherwise use find_user_id_by_name_zip with first_name, last_name, and zip before proceeding.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show users sometimes provide both email and name+zip (e.g., task 109); the playbook's 'exactly one' requirement is too strict and assistants already prefer using email when available.

all_deltas.content: IF no explicit user confirmation is received after a proposed change → THEN do not execute exchange/return/modify/cancel actions (ask for explicit 'Yes' to proceed). If, however, the user explicitly requests the change (i.e., directly asks the agent to perform the action rather than merely responding to a proposal), the agent may proceed without an additional 'Yes' confirmation.
all_deltas.rationale: Some conversations show users explicitly requesting actions (start a return/exchange) and the agent proceeding without an extra 'Yes' prompt; the rule should distinguish between agent-proposed changes and user-initiated requests (tasks 92 and 8).

all_deltas.content: IF a user asks to update their account/default address and/or a pending order address by using the address from one of their orders (and prefers not to provide the address directly) → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) to retrieve the order's shipping address, present that address to the user (or proceed if the user explicitly authorized using the order address), and then call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) and modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) as requested.
all_deltas.rationale: Users may ask to use an address from an order as their new account/default address; conversation 109 demonstrates retrieving an order address and updating both account and pending order addresses via modify_user_address and modify_pending_order_address.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests returns or exchanges that include items from multiple orders → THEN handle each order separately: call get_order_details(order_id) for each order involved, and call return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items per order using the payment_method_id from that order's payment_history (ensure each tool call references the correct order and payment method).
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories (tasks 92 and 91) show users returning/exchanging items that span multiple orders; processing must be done per order and use each order's payment method for refunds/charges.

all_deltas.content: IF a user provides only a single name (for example, only a first name) together with a zip code → THEN ask for the full name (first and last) before attempting a name+zip lookup.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents asked for a full name when the user only provided a first name and zip (Conversation 1). The playbook should require requesting a last name in that case to reliably find the user.

all_deltas.content: IF an order status is pending AND the user requests an address update → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the shipping address before fulfillment.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents updated the shipping address for a pending order using modify_pending_order_address (Conversation 4). The playbook lacks a guidance rule for pending-order address changes.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests an action that is disallowed by policy (for example, refunds to a different payment method) OR explicitly requests a human agent or escalation → THEN refuse the disallowed action per policy and offer/escalate to human agents using transfer_to_human_agents(summary) when the user requests it or when appropriate.
all_deltas.rationale: When users request disallowed refund routing or demand a human, agents escalated to human agents (Conversation 1). The playbook should explicitly allow/require escalation in these cases.

all_deltas.content: IF cancelling a pending order → THEN ask for the cancellation reason (e.g., 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') prior to cancelling. A clear user instruction with a stated reason may be treated as confirmation to proceed with the cancellation.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents asked for a cancellation reason and proceeded when the user replied with a clear reason (Conversation 2). The playbook should require capturing the reason and allow that explicit instruction to serve as confirmation.

all_deltas.content: IF no explicit user confirmation is received after a proposed change → THEN do not execute exchange/return/modify/cancel actions. EXCEPTION: treat an unambiguous user instruction to perform the action (for example: 'Cancel this order', 'No longer needed', 'Please modify to X') or a clearly stated cancellation reason as sufficient confirmation to proceed without an additional 'Yes' prompt.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents proceeded with cancellations when users gave an unambiguous instruction with a reason rather than replying explicitly 'Yes' (Conversation 2). The rule is updated to allow such clear instructions to count as confirmation.

IF a user requests returns and exchanges in the same request → THEN do not execute both actions simultaneously; ask the user to choose which action to perform first (process them as separate requests).

IF a user requests an update to their account shipping address → THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the account address. IF a user requests shipping-address updates for orders → THEN only modify addresses for orders with status 'pending' using modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip). When performing these updates, communicate which orders were successfully updated and which could not be (e.g., processed/delivered orders).

IF changing a user's account address → THEN note that this does NOT retroactively change the shipping address on already processed or delivered orders; order shipping addresses must be updated separately and only pending orders can have their shipping address modified. The assistant must inform the user which orders (by order id) were updated and which orders could not be updated due to status.

IF order status is pending AND user requests an address update → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the shipping address. Before executing, present the exact new address that will be applied (or state 'use address on file' if requested) and ask for explicit confirmation from the user.

IF an exchange or modification requires charging a payment method that may have limited balance (e.g., a gift card) → THEN check the payment_method's available balance (from get_user_details/payment method metadata) before executing. If the balance is insufficient to cover any required charge, DO NOT execute the exchange/modification. Inform the user of the insufficient balance, provide the exact shortfall amount, and offer options: (a) provide an alternate payment method, (b) pause/hold the requested change until the user supplies payment, or (c) cancel the change request. Always use the original payment method for refunds.

IF an order contains multiple similar or identical items (duplicate item_ids or same product repeated) AND the user references 'one of them' or is ambiguous about which unit to act on → THEN ask the user to specify which item they mean (by item options, item_id index, shipping address, or other distinguishing detail) before proceeding with returns/exchanges/modifications.

IF a user requests to change the shipping address for a pending order → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update that order's shipping address. IF the user also asks to update their account default address (or requests the account address to match an order's shipping address) → THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) after confirming with the user.

IF a user requests multiple changes (exchanges/modifications/returns) and only confirms some of the proposed replacements/options → THEN proceed only with the actions the user explicitly confirmed (execute tools for confirmed items) and leave unconfirmed items unchanged; confirm the exact actions and payment method for the confirmed subset before executing.

IF order status is pending AND user requests a shipping address change → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the order's shipping address.
IF find_user_id_by_name_zip(...) returns no user → THEN ask the user for their email and call find_user_id_by_email(email) to attempt to locate the account as a fallback.

IF an order's status is cancelled → THEN the order cannot be reinstated via automated return/modify/cancel/exchange tools; refuse automated reinstatement and escalate to human agents (transfer_to_human_agents) for manual review/possible reinstatement.

IF a user provides a customer ID / user_id directly → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve the user's email, payment methods, address, and list of order IDs (accept the provided user_id as valid identifying info in lieu of name+zip or email).

IF a user requests to split payment across multiple payment methods for a single order → THEN refuse the request and inform the user that a single payment method must be used for that order; offer alternatives such as removing items to reduce the total, using a different single payment method, or placing a new separate order to use a different card.

IF an order status is pending AND the user requests cancellation of only some items in that pending order → THEN do not perform partial cancellations; pending orders may be modified (change variants/quantities) using modify_pending_order_items or cancelled in full using cancel_pending_order. Partial-item returns/cancellations are only allowed for delivered orders.

IF the user requests multiple returns/exchanges across multiple orders → THEN compute and clearly present the aggregated total refund or total charge (net) and list which payment method(s) each amount will be applied to (e.g., 'Total refund would be $X to your [payment method(s)]'), and confirm with the user.

IF order status is pending AND user requests an address change → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the shipping address before performing other pending-order actions; present the updated address to the user and ask for explicit confirmation.

IF a user requests an aggregate total or spending summary for a product type/category across their past orders → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to obtain order IDs, call get_order_details(order_id) for each order, filter items by the requested product_id(s) or product name, sum the item prices, and present the total with an optional breakdown by order and item.

IF multiple exchanges/returns/modifications are requested across one or more orders → THEN present a per-order breakdown listing order_id, old item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or return/cancel), per-item and per-order price(s), the price difference for each order, the payment_method_id to be used for each charge/refund, and the total net charge or refund across all orders; require explicit user confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue') before executing any tool calls.

DO NOT request identifying information (name/zip/email) for general product inquiries, browsing, or availability requests. IF order status is pending AND user requests a shipping address change → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the shipping address for the pending order and present the updated shipping details to the user for confirmation.

IF an order already has an active return_requested or exchange_requested state → THEN do not initiate additional return or exchange requests for the same order until the existing request is resolved; inform the user of the existing request and next steps.

IF a payment_method of type gift_card is used for a modification or pending-order charge → THEN deduct the charged amount from the gift_card balance, update the user's payment_methods balance, and report the remaining gift card balance to the user as part of the confirmation communication after the action is prepared and/or executed.

IF a user requests cancellation for only part of a pending order → THEN inform the user that partial cancellations are not supported (or per-system policy), and offer the appropriate alternative (use modify_pending_order_items to remove/replace items if supported, or cancel the entire order if the system supports it).

Confirm which specific orders the user wants changed and only modify orders with modifiable statuses (see precondition rules).

IF an address modification request is received → THEN check the order status: delivered orders' shipping addresses cannot be changed; only pending/processed (modifiable) orders can have their shipping address updated. Inform the user which orders cannot be changed and proceed only for eligible orders.

IF a user asks a question or makes a request that is unrelated to their account, orders, returns, or exchanges → THEN do not request identifying information (name/zip/email). Only request identifying info when it is needed to look up or act on the user's account/orders.

IF a user is unsure which payment method they used for an order → THEN retrieve the order details and read payment_history to identify the original payment_method_id to use for refunds/charges instead of asking the user to supply the payment method.

If the balance is insufficient to cover any required charge, DO NOT execute the exchange/modification. Inform the user of the insufficient balance, provide the exact shortfall amount, and offer options: (a) provide an alternate payment method, (b) pause/hold the requested change until the user supplies payment, or (c) cancel the change request. Always use the original payment method for refunds.

IF an order contains multiple similar or identical items (duplicate item_ids or same product repeated) AND the user references 'one of them' or is ambiguous about which unit to act on → THEN ask the user to specify which item they mean (by item options, item_id index, shipping address, or other distinguishing detail) before proceeding with returns/exchanges/modifications.

IF a user requests multiple changes (exchanges/modifications/returns) and only confirms some of the proposed replacements/options → THEN proceed only with the actions the user explicitly confirmed (execute tools for confirmed items) and leave unconfirmed items unchanged; confirm the exact actions and payment method for the confirmed subset before executing.

IF order status is pending AND the user requests a shipping/address change → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the order's shipping address before any other pending-order actions or fulfillment; after updating, call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm the new address and any price/shipping changes, present the updated shipping details and order summary to the user, and ask for explicit confirmation.
IF find_user_id_by_name_zip(...) returns no user → THEN ask the user for their email and call find_user_id_by_email(email) to attempt to locate the account as a fallback.

IF an order's status is cancelled → THEN the order cannot be reinstated via automated return/modify/cancel/exchange tools; refuse automated reinstatement and escalate to human agents (transfer_to_human_agents) for manual review/possible reinstatement.

IF a user provides a customer ID / user_id directly → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve the user's email, payment methods, address, and list of order IDs (accept the provided user_id as valid identifying info in lieu of name+zip or email).

IF a user requests to split payment across multiple payment methods for a single order → THEN refuse the request and inform the user that a single payment method must be used for that order; offer alternatives such as removing items to reduce the total, using a different single payment method, or placing a new separate order to use a different card.

IF an order status is pending AND the user requests cancellation of only some items in that pending order → THEN do not perform partial cancellations; pending orders may be modified (change variants/quantities) using modify_pending_order_items or cancelled in full using cancel_pending_order. Partial-item returns/cancellations are only allowed for delivered orders.

IF the user requests multiple returns/exchanges across multiple orders → THEN compute and clearly present the aggregated total refund or total charge (net) and list which payment method(s) each amount will be applied to (e.g., 'Total refund would be $X to your [payment method(s)]'), and confirm with the user.

IF a user requests an aggregate total or spending summary for a product type/category across their past orders → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to obtain order IDs, call get_order_details(order_id) for each order, filter items by the requested product_id(s) or product name, sum the item prices, and present the total with an optional breakdown by order and item.

IF multiple exchanges/returns/modifications are requested across one or more orders → THEN present a per-order breakdown listing order_id, old item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or return/cancel), per-item and per-order price(s), the price difference for each order, the payment_method_id to be used for each charge/refund, and the total net charge or refund across all orders; require explicit user confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue') before executing any tool calls.

DO NOT request identifying information (name/zip/email) for general product inquiries, browsing, or availability requests. Only prompt for identifying info when the user requests account-specific actions (account/order changes, returns, exchanges, cancellations, or other actions that require accessing the user's account or orders).

IF an order already has an active return_requested or exchange_requested state → THEN do not initiate additional return or exchange requests for the same order until the existing request is resolved; inform the user of the existing request and next steps.

IF a payment_method of type gift_card is used for a modification or pending-order charge → THEN deduct the charged amount from the gift_card balance, update the user's payment_methods balance, and report the remaining gift card balance to the user as part of the confirmation communication after the action is prepared and/or executed.

IF a user requests cancellation for only part of a pending order → THEN inform the user that partial cancellations are not supported (or per-system policy), and offer the appropriate alternative (use modify_pending_order_items to remove/replace items if supported, or cancel the entire order if the system supports it).

IF an address modification request is received → THEN check the order status: delivered orders' shipping addresses cannot be changed; only pending/processed (modifiable) orders can have their shipping address updated. Inform the user which orders cannot be changed and proceed only for eligible orders.

IF a user asks a question or makes a request that is unrelated to their account, orders, returns, or exchanges → THEN do not request identifying information (name/zip/email). Only request identifying info when it is needed to look up or act on the user's account/orders.

IF a user is unsure which payment method they used for an order → THEN retrieve the order details and read payment_history to identify the original payment_method_id to use for refunds/charges instead of asking the user to supply the payment method.

IF a user requests refunds/returns to be applied to a different payment method than the original order's payment_history → THEN refuse and state that refunds/returns must go to the original payment method for that order, and IF the user insists or requests further assistance → THEN offer to escalate/transfer the request to a human agent for review.

IF requested returns/exchanges include items from more than one order → THEN handle each order separately: call get_order_details(order_id) for each order involved, and for each order call return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items using the payment_method_id from that order's payment_history for refunds/charges, ensuring each tool call references the correct order and payment method.
IF a user provides only a single name (for example, only a first name) together with a zip code → THEN ask for the full name (first and last) before attempting a name+zip lookup.

IF a user requests an action that is disallowed by policy (for example, refunds to a different payment method) OR explicitly requests a human agent or escalation → THEN refuse the disallowed action per policy and offer/escalate to human agents using transfer_to_human_agents(summary) when the user requests it or when appropriate.

IF cancelling a pending order → THEN ask for the cancellation reason (e.g., 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') prior to cancelling. A clear user instruction with a stated reason may be treated as confirmation to proceed with the cancellation.

## Information Gathering

  [info-006] IF a product variant or compatibility/option is needed to propose an exchange or modification → THEN call list_all_product_types (to find product_id) and get_product_details(product_id) to inspect variant item_ids, options, availability, and price
  [info-007] IF an order contains multiple similar or identical items (duplicate item_ids or same product repeated) AND the user references 'one of them' or is ambiguous about which unit to act on → THEN ask the user to specify which item they mean (by item options, item_id index, shipping address, or other distinguishing detail) before proceeding with returns/exchanges/modifications.
  [info-008] IF find_user_id_by_name_zip(...) returns no user → THEN ask the user for their email and call find_user_id_by_email(email) to attempt to locate the account as a fallback.

  [info-010] IF a user requests an aggregate total or spending summary for a product type/category across their past orders → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to obtain order IDs, call get_order_details(order_id) for each order, filter items by the requested product_id(s) or product name, sum the item prices, and present the total with an optional breakdown by order and item.
  [info-011] DO NOT request identifying information (name/zip/email) for general product inquiries, browsing, or availability requests. Only prompt for identifying info when the user requests account-specific actions (account/order changes, returns, exchanges, cancellations, or other actions that require accessing the user's account or orders).
  [info-012] IF a user asks a question or makes a request that is unrelated to their account, orders, returns, or exchanges → THEN do not request identifying information (name/zip/email). Only request identifying info when it is needed to look up or act on the user's account/orders.
  [info-013] IF a user is unsure which payment method they used for an order → THEN retrieve the order details and read payment_history to identify the original payment_method_id to use for refunds/charges instead of asking the user to supply the payment method.
  [info-014] IF a user provides only a single name (for example, only a first name) together with a zip code → THEN ask for the full name (first and last) before attempting a name+zip lookup.

## Action Execution
  [acti-001] IF order status is delivered AND user requests an exchange → THEN use exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id) to place the exchange
  [acti-002] IF order status is delivered AND user requests a return/refund → THEN use return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id) and set payment_method_id to the original payment method from the order's payment_history
  [acti-003] IF order status is pending AND user requests a size/variant change → THEN use modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id) to update the pending order
  [acti-004] IF order status is pending AND user requests cancellation → THEN use cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) to cancel and issue refund to the original payment method. Note: cancel_pending_order applies to the entire pending order; partial-item cancellations on pending orders are not supported. If the user requests cancelling only some items, inform them that partial cancellation of a pending order isn't available and offer alternatives (cancel the entire order, or modify/replace items if supported while pending).
  [acti-005] IF proposing a replacement item or variant → THEN select new_item_ids only from get_product_details variants where available == true
  [acti-006] IF a price difference exists for an exchange or modification → THEN charge or refund using the payment_method_id supplied (typically the original payment method) and include payment_method_id in the tool call
  [acti-007] IF a user explicitly requests refunds to a different payment method → THEN verify the requested payment_method_id belongs to the same user (exists in get_user_details). If it does and system policy permits applying refunds/charges to that method (e.g., for exchanges/price differences), honor the request. Refuse requests that attempt refunds to payment methods not in the user's account, to another customer's payment method, or to payment methods that the system does not support for refunds.
  [acti-008] IF a user requests an address change for their account and/or one or more orders → THEN present the exact new address that will be applied (or state "use address on file" if requested) and ask for explicit confirmation before executing. If the user asks to use an address from one of their orders, call get_order_details(order_id), present the retrieved shipping address to the user (and proceed only with explicit authorization to use it). For account address updates call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip). For order updates, only modify orders with modifiable statuses (e.g., status 'pending') by calling modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) for each specified order. Confirm with the user which specific orders to change, perform the permitted updates, then report which orders were successfully updated and which could not be changed (e.g., already processed/delivered). If the user also requests the account default address be updated to match an order or the new address, update the account after confirming with the user. Finally, present the updated address(es) to the user and confirm completion.
  [acti-012] IF a user requests multiple changes (exchanges/modifications/returns) and only confirms some of the proposed replacements/options → THEN proceed only with the actions the user explicitly confirmed (execute tools for confirmed items) and leave unconfirmed items unchanged; confirm the exact actions and payment method for the confirmed subset before executing.
  [acti-013] IF order status is pending AND the user requests a shipping/address change → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the order's shipping address before any other pending-order actions or fulfillment; after updating, call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm the new address and any price/shipping changes, present the updated shipping details and order summary to the user, and ask for explicit confirmation.
  [acti-014] IF a user requests to split payment across multiple payment methods for a single order → THEN refuse the request and inform the user that a single payment method must be used for that order; offer alternatives such as removing items to reduce the total, using a different single payment method, or placing a new separate order to use a different card.

  [acti-019] IF a user requests refunds/returns to be applied to a different payment method than the original order's payment_history → THEN refuse and state that refunds/returns must go to the original payment method for that order, and IF the user insists or requests further assistance → THEN offer to escalate/transfer the request to a human agent for review.
  [acti-021] IF requested returns/exchanges include items from more than one order → THEN handle each order separately: call get_order_details(order_id) for each order involved, and for each order call return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items using the payment_method_id from that order's payment_history for refunds/charges, ensuring each tool call references the correct order and payment method.
  [acti-026] IF a user requests an action that is disallowed by policy (for example, refunds to a different payment method) OR explicitly requests a human agent or escalation → THEN refuse the disallowed action per policy and offer/escalate to human agents using transfer_to_human_agents(summary) when the user requests it or when appropriate.

## Confirmation Communication
  [conf-001] IF preparing to execute a change (exchange, return, modification, cancellation) → THEN present the exact actions to the user including order id, old item_ids, proposed new item_ids (or return/cancel), price(s), and payment_method_id to be used and ask for explicit confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue'). If the user has already provided an unambiguous, explicit instruction authorizing the requested change earlier in the conversation (for example, they asked to proceed with the specific exchange/modification and specified preferences), restate the exact actions being taken and proceed without an additional 'Yes' only if the prior message clearly authorized proceeding.
  [conf-002] IF an exchange/return results in a price difference → THEN communicate the exact refund or charge amount and the payment method it will be applied to (for refunds: 'refund would be $X to your [payment method]') after executing the action
  [conf-003] IF an order will ship later → THEN tell the user they will receive an email when it ships
  [conf-004] AFTER completing requested actions via tools → THEN send a short confirmation message (e.g., 'Done!', 'All done') and ask if anything else is needed
  [conf-005] IF the user requests multiple returns/exchanges across multiple orders → THEN compute and clearly present the aggregated total refund or total charge (net) and list which payment method(s) each amount will be applied to (e.g., 'Total refund would be $X to your [payment method(s)]'), and confirm with the user.
  [conf-006] IF multiple exchanges/returns/modifications are requested across one or more orders → THEN present a per-order breakdown listing order_id, old item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or return/cancel), per-item and per-order price(s), the price difference for each order, the payment_method_id to be used for each charge/refund, and the total net charge or refund across all orders; require explicit user confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue') before executing any tool calls.
  [conf-007] IF cancelling a pending order → THEN ask for the cancellation reason (e.g., 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') prior to cancelling. A clear user instruction with a stated reason may be treated as confirmation to proceed with the cancellation.

## Precondition Rules
  [prec-001] IF an action involves returned or exchanged items → THEN verify the order's status: delivered orders may be returned or exchanged; pending orders must be modified or cancelled; cancelled orders cannot be returned or exchanged. (Do not use delivered-return/exchange tools on pending or cancelled orders.)
  [prec-002] IF modifying/exchanging an item → THEN ensure the replacement variant's availability is true in get_product_details before proposing it
  [prec-003] IF performing any charge or refund → THEN ensure the specified payment_method_id exists in get_user_details(payment_methods). For refunds, default to the original payment method from the order's payment_history. However, if the customer explicitly requests a different payment method that exists in their account and the system supports applying the charge/refund to that method (e.g., for exchanges or price differences), honor the customer's request and use the specified payment_method_id, documenting the selection in the tool call.
  [prec-004] IF no explicit user confirmation is received after a proposed change → THEN do not execute exchange/return/modify/cancel actions. EXCEPTION: treat an unambiguous user instruction to perform the action (for example: 'Cancel this order', 'No longer needed', 'Please modify to X') or a clearly stated cancellation reason as sufficient confirmation to proceed without an additional 'Yes' prompt.

actions.old_content: IF preparing to execute a change (exchange, return, modification, cancellation) → THEN present the exact actions to the user including order id, old item_ids, proposed new item_ids (or return/cancel), price(s), and payment_method_id to be used and ask for explicit confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue')

If the user has already provided an unambiguous, explicit instruction authorizing the requested change earlier in the conversation (for example, they asked to proceed with the specific exchange/modification and specified preferences), restate the exact actions being taken and proceed without an additional 'Yes' only if the prior message clearly authorized proceeding.
